page: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g-1920s is a general-purpose virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC processor with 32 shared vCPUs, derived from 16 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a base speed of 2.0 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 1920 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing placing in the top 10% tier. However, due to the shared CPU allocation model, multi-core scaling is more moderate, with stress-ng multi-core performance landing in the middle 50% average. Memory performance is highly competitive; the Passmark memory score and large-block uncached memory write bandwidth both rank in the top 10% tier. Cryptographic and compression workloads perform well, with single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reaching top 10% speeds. Database operations are also robust, with Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations scoring in the top 25% tier.

This server configuration offers high resource density, combining a substantial 128.0 GB memory footprint and a large 1920 GB NVMe SSD on a shared-core infrastructure. This makes it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring significant memory and storage capacity without needing dedicated CPU execution threads. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, database hosting, caching layers, and lightweight LLM inference, where the system's top-tier small-model text generation speeds can be fully utilized.
description: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g-1920s is a general-purpose virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, 128.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 1920 GB local NVMe SSD. While its shared CPU allocation results in average multi-core scaling, it delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and exceptional memory write bandwidth. The hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for resource-dense deployments, offering a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio alongside substantial local storage. This server is well-suited for database operations, memory-intensive caching, static web serving, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g-1920s is a general-purpose server featuring 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for database hosting and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g-1920s is an x86_64 server with 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB NVMe SSD for database and web serving workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity shared-CPU virtual server combining 128 GB of RAM and 1.9 TB of NVMe storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
128.0 GB of system memory and 1920 GB local NVMe SSD
Top-tier single-core CPU and memory write performance
Cost-efficient shared-resource model for high-density deployments
Suitable for database hosting, caching, and static web serving
